task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface of the skull
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D rendered model of a skull is visible in the main 3D viewer window of the medical imaging application.

task_completion.description: Set the 3D background color to pure green
task_completion.visual_evidence: The background of the 3D viewer window is a solid, bright green color (chroma key green).
There is no bounding box or wireframe cube surrounding the 3D skull in the viewer.
There are no orientation markers (such as a small cube, axis arrows, or letters like A, P, S, I, R, L) visible in the corners of the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the 3D view to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal confirms that an image file named 'chroma_skull.png' has been saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the medical imaging software's GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the application's menus, view controllers, or volume rendering modules rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The 3D render is genuinely generated from the loaded DICOM scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface shows the CT Cranium volume loaded in the data or volume modules, and the 3D view corresponds to this medical data, not a pre-existing stock image.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application to create a dummy image file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot was captured using the application's screen capture functionality or system screenshot tools, rather than using terminal commands (like 'touch' or 'convert') to create a fake image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/chroma_skull.png'. The expected background color in the 3D view is pure green, RGB(0, 255, 0).
